page: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-32 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical Intel cores with hyper-threading enabled. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 256 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. The processor features a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache, alongside advanced instruction sets including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions and Advanced Vector Extensions. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates strong memory subsystem performance, with uncached memory read bandwidth reaching 208.51 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 99.59 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% tier. The instance also demonstrates strong capabilities in static web serving throughput at 44.59 GB/sec and image processing. CPU-bound tasks, such as stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench compiling, perform in the average range. For machine learning workloads, the CPU's AMX instructions support top-tier text generation speeds of 328.98 tokens/sec for small 135M LLM models, though performance scales down to average levels for larger 7B and 70B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n4-highmem-32 provides a high-density memory configuration without the added expense of local NVMe storage or discrete GPU hardware. This makes it a balanced choice for workloads that require substantial memory capacity and strong memory bandwidth but do not need local disk caching. The instance is well-suited for memory-intensive applications such as in-memory databases, Redis caching layers, enterprise web serving, text and image processing pipelines, and small-scale language model inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-32 is a memory-optimized instance configured with 32 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 256 GB of RAM, establishing an 8 GB per vCPU ratio. Built on the x86_64 architecture, the CPU features a 260 MB L3 cache and supports Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions and AVX-512. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs. In benchmark testing, it delivers strong uncached memory read bandwidth of 208.51 GB/sec and write bandwidth of 99.59 GB/sec, alongside strong static web serving throughput. CPU performance is average overall, though it achieves top-tier text generation speeds for small-scale LLM inference. This hardware profile is optimized for memory-intensive workloads, including in-memory databases, Redis deployments, text processing, and enterprise web serving where high memory density is required without local storage overhead.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-32 is a memory-optimized instance with 32 Intel vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and x86_64 architecture. It features strong memory bandwidth and is optimized for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-32 features 32 Intel vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and strong memory bandwidth for database and caching workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 virtual machine featuring high memory bandwidth and advanced matrix extensions for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 256 GB of system memory
High memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core
Strong uncached memory read and write bandwidth
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction set support
Best suited for in-memory databases, caching, and web serving
